note: Verified key claims against PyTorch codebase

scores.justification: Right diagnosis (warmup + CPU) but assumes Colab context; misses small-model inherent limitation
Correctly IDs Windows CPP builder limitation; workaround validity (config.cpp.cxx to MSVC) unverified
Plausible cumprod decomposition diagnosis, valid torch.compiler.disable workaround
Correctly IDs no canary mode; lists valid tools (TORCH_COMPILE_DEBUG, profiler, binary search)
Valid debugging workflow but gives methodology, not actual fix
Right area (non-deterministic kernels) but reduce-overhead for determinism is questionable
Correct: dataclass limitation, valid workarounds, accurate 2.1+ version guidance
FABRICATED: claims cache keys include timestamps or object IDs. Codebase confirms FxGraphCachePickler explicitly avoids this
Correct: max-autotune selects kernels with different numerical properties; allow_tf32=False valid fix
Correctly IDs two integration paths and missing register_fake as root cause
WRONG: claims dynamic=True only marks batch dim. Codebase confirms it makes ALL dims dynamic
Valid debugging steps but recommends suppress_errors=True — dangerous, masks real bugs
Correct: regional compilation, avoid max-autotune, enable FX graph cache
Right area (FX cache = Inductor only) but no frontend cache exists may be outdated
Correct: torch.library.custom_op + register_fake, valid tutorial ref
WRONG claim no torch.scan equivalent — torch._higher_order_ops.scan exists (prototype). Listed alternatives real but missed direct answer
Plausible SDPA/GQA diagnosis, valid workarounds, but specific trigger conditions read as fabricated
Practical advice works but diagnosis wrong — PyTorch handles this by design via graph breaks, not misclassification
Solid practical advice (dynamic=True, padding) but shallow on GNN-specific challenges
Right area (buffer aliasing + mutates_args); non-mutating ops workaround is practical
